page: Amazon Web Services i8g.16xlarge is an arm64-based storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 15000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with top-tier rankings in memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached operations. The server demonstrates high efficiency in data-centric tasks, achieving top-tier performance in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average category, while LLM text generation benchmarks show top-tier results for medium-sized models but weaker performance on smaller models.

Given its high local storage capacity, dedicated arm64 cores, and top-tier memory bandwidth, the i8g.16xlarge offers high resource density for data-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, data decompression pipelines, and memory-bound analytical applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.16xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 512.0 GB of RAM and 15000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, Redis SET transactions, and multi-threaded decompression, while single-core performance remains average. This configuration provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive environments. The server is optimized for high-throughput databases, memory-bound analytics, and large-scale web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.16xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server with 64 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 15000 GB NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.16xlarge is an arm64 storage-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated cores, 512 GB RAM, and 15000 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An arm64 storage-optimized server featuring sixty-four dedicated cores, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 arm64 cores running at 2.8 GHz
15000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
512.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis transactions
Optimized for large-scale databases, analytics, and web serving
